speaker: doctor
text: Good morning. Based on your recent evaluation, I’m diagnosing you with Coronary Artery Disease, confirmed as of September 9th.

speaker: doctor
text: Your systolic blood pressure today is 177 mmHg — that’s elevated and something we’ll need to manage closely.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, I’ve been feeling a bit short of breath when climbing stairs lately.

speaker: doctor
text: That’s consistent with CAD. Have you noticed any chest discomfort or dizziness?

speaker: doctor
text: Your body temperature is normal at 36.1 °C — no sign of acute infection today.

speaker: patient
text: No fever, but I’ve had some swelling in my ankles by evening.

speaker: doctor
text: Your hemoglobin level is 145 g/L — well within the normal range, which is reassuring for cardiac oxygen delivery.

speaker: doctor
text: Are you currently taking any medications for diabetes or hypertension?

speaker: patient
text: Yes — I’ve been extremely tired lately, even after eight hours of sleep. Just constant fatigue.

speaker: doctor
text: I see you’re on Metformin 1000mg twice daily for type 2 diabetes — we’ll keep that unchanged for now.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve also been taking Lisinopril 10mg every morning for blood pressure, but it hasn’t brought my numbers down much.

speaker: doctor
text: Given your persistent elevation, I’m going to switch you from Lisinopril to Amlodipine 5mg once daily — it’s more effective for your current profile.

speaker: patient
text: Okay, I’ll start that tomorrow. Any side effects I should watch for?

speaker: doctor
text: Mild ankle swelling can occur — but since you already have some, we’ll monitor closely and adjust if needed.

speaker: patient
text: Also, my home BP readings have been varying — sometimes over 180.

speaker: doctor
text: Let’s review your technique and consider ambulatory monitoring. Consistency matters for treatment decisions.

speaker: patient
text: Makes sense. I’ll bring my log next time.

speaker: doctor
text: Before we wrap up — any new symptoms or concerns since our last check-in?

speaker: doctor
text: Just rechecked your blood pressure — it’s now 162 mmHg, which is an improvement but still above target.

speaker: patient
text: That’s interesting — my temperature is still 36.1 °C, same as earlier, and my hemoglobin came back at 145 g/L again, just like the first reading.
